message.content: 深度分析模块 art-runtime（路径: android/art/runtime/）。识别关键文件、类、函数，理解模块职责和对外接口。返回详细的模块分析。描述: ART 运行时对象模型

attachment.content:
- coredump: Analyze an Android coredump file. Print a Java class and its members, or drill into any field by address.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- superpowers:receiving-code-review
- superpowers:requesting-code-review
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- superpowers:systematic-debugging
- superpowers:test-driven-development
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees
- superpowers:using-superpowers
- superpowers:verification-before-completion
- superpowers:writing-plans
- superpowers:writing-skills
- claude-mem:babysit
- claude-mem:design-is
- claude-mem:do
- claude-mem:how-it-works
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent
- claude-mem:learn-codebase
- claude-mem:make-plan
- claude-mem:mem-search
- claude-mem:oh-my-issues
- claude-mem:pathfinder
- claude-mem:smart-explore
- claude-mem:timeline-report
- claude-mem:version-bump
- claude-mem:weekly-digests
- claude-mem:wowerpoint
- understand-anything:understand
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: 我将深度分析 `android/art/runtime/` 模块。让我先查看该目录的结构和关键文件。
现在让我读取关键文件来深度理解这个模块。

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * Copyright (C) 2024-present, Guanyou.Chen. All rights reserved.
3	 *
4	 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	 * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	 * You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	 *
8	 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	 *
10	 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	 * limitations under the License.
15	 */
16	
17	#ifndef ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_STACK_H_
18	#define ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_STACK_H_
19	
20	#include "runtime/java_frame.h"
21	#include "runtime/thread.h"
22	#include "runtime/managed_stack.h"
23	#include "runtime/quick/quick_method_frame_info.h"
24	#include <memory>
25	
26	/*
27	 * Our current stack layout.
28	 * The Dalvik registers come first, followed by the
29	 * Method*, followed by other special temporaries if any, followed by
30	 * regular compiler temporary. As of now we only have the Method* as
31	 * as a special compiler temporary.
32	 * A compiler temporary can be thought of as a virtual register that
33	 * does not exist in the dex but holds intermediate values to help
34	 * optimizations and code generation. A special compiler temporary is
35	 * one whose location in frame is well known while non-special ones
36	 * do not have a requirement on location in frame as long as code
37	 * generator itself knows how to access them.
38	 *
39	 *     +-------------------------------+
40	 *     | IN[ins-1]                     |  {Note: resides in caller's frame}
41	 *     |       .                       |
42	 *     | IN[0]                         |
43	 *     | caller's ArtMethod            |  ... ArtMethod*
44	 *     +===============================+  {Note: start of callee's frame}
45	 *     | core callee-save spill        |  {variable sized}
46	 *     +-------------------------------+
47	 *     | fp callee-save spill          |
48	 *     +-------------------------------+
49	 *     | filler word                   |  {For compatibility, if V[locals-1] used as wide
50	 *     +-------------------------------+
51	 *     | V[locals-1]                   |
52	 *     | V[locals-2]                   |
53	 *     |      .                        |
54	 *     |      .                        |  ... (reg == 2)
55	 *     | V[1]                          |  ... (reg == 1)
56	 *     | V[0]                          |  ... (reg == 0) <---- "locals_start"
57	 *     +-------------------------------+
58	 *     | stack alignment padding       |  {0 to (kStackAlignWords-1) of padding}
59	 *     +-------------------------------+
60	 *     | Compiler temp region          |  ... (reg >= max_num_special_temps)
61	 *     |      .                        |
62	 *     |      .                        |
63	 *     | V[max_num_special_temps + 1]  |
64	 *     | V[max_num_special_temps + 0]  |
65	 *     +-------------------------------+
66	 *     | OUT[outs-1]                   |
67	 *     | OUT[outs-2]                   |
68	 *     |       .                       |
69	 *     | OUT[0]                        |
70	 *     | ArtMethod*                    |  ... (reg == num_total_code_regs == special_temp_value) <<== sp, 16-byte aligned
71	 *     +===============================+
72	 */
73	
74	namespace art {
75	
76	class StackVisitor {
77	public:
78	    // This enum defines a flag to control whether inlined frames are included
79	    // when walking the stack.
80	    enum class StackWalkKind {
81	        kIncludeInlinedFrames,
82	        kSkipInlinedFrames,
83	    };
84	
85	    StackVisitor(Thread* thread, StackWalkKind kind) : thread_(thread), walk_kind_(kind) {}
86	    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<JavaFrame>>& GetJavaFrames() { return java_frames_; }
87	    Thread* GetThread() { return thread_; }
88	    void WalkStack();
89	    bool VisitFrame();
90	    ArtMethod GetMethod();
91	    ArtMethod GetTopMethod();
92	    QuickMethodFrameInfo GetQuickFrameInfo(QuickFrame& quick_frame);
93	    ~StackVisitor() { java_frames_.clear(); }
94	private:
95	    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<JavaFrame>> java_frames_;
96	    Thread* thread_;
97	    const StackWalkKind walk_kind_;
98	    ShadowFrame cur_shadow_frame_ = 0x0;
99	    QuickFrame cur_quick_frame_ = 0x0;
100	    QuickFrame prev_quick_frame_ = 0x0;
101	};
102	
103	} // namespace art
104	
105	#endif  // ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_STACK_H_
106

content.text: 我将继续深入分析其他关键文件。
让我继续查看 JIT 和锁相关的关键实现文件。

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * Copyright (C) 2024-present, Guanyou.Chen. All rights reserved.
3	 *
4	 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	 * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	 * You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	 *
8	 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	 *
10	 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	 * limitations under the License.
15	 */
16	
17	#ifndef ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_LOCK_WORD_H_
18	#define ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_LOCK_WORD_H_
19	
20	#include "runtime/monitor.h"
21	#include "runtime/runtime_globals.h"
22	
23	namespace art {
24	
25	class LockWord {
26	public:
27	    enum SizeShiftsAndMasks : uint32_t {  // private marker to avoid generate-operator-out.py from processing.
28	        // Number of bits to encode the state, currently just fat or thin/unlocked or hash code.
29	        kStateSize = 2,
30	        kReadBarrierStateSize = 1,
31	        kMarkBitStateSize = 1,
32	        // Number of bits to encode the thin lock owner.
33	        kThinLockOwnerSize = 16,
34	        // Remaining bits are the recursive lock count. Zero means it is locked exactly once
35	        // and not recursively.
36	        kThinLockCountSize = 32 - kThinLockOwnerSize - kStateSize - kReadBarrierStateSize -
37	            kMarkBitStateSize,
38	
39	        // Thin lock bits. Owner in lowest bits.
40	        kThinLockOwnerShift = 0,
41	        kThinLockOwnerMask = (1 << kThinLockOwnerSize) - 1,
42	        kThinLockOwnerMaskShifted = kThinLockOwnerMask << kThinLockOwnerShift,
43	        kThinLockMaxOwner = kThinLockOwnerMask,
44	        // Count in higher bits.
45	        kThinLockCountShift = kThinLockOwnerSize + kThinLockOwnerShift,
46	        kThinLockCountMask = (1 << kThinLockCountSize) - 1,
47	        kThinLockMaxCount = kThinLockCountMask,
48	        kThinLockCountOne = 1 << kThinLockCountShift,  // == 65536 (0x10000)
49	        kThinLockCountMaskShifted = kThinLockCountMask << kThinLockCountShift,
50	
51	        // State in the highest bits.
52	        kStateShift = kReadBarrierStateSize + kThinLockCountSize + kThinLockCountShift +
53	            kMarkBitStateSize,
54	        kStateMask = (1 << kStateSize) - 1,
55	        kStateMaskShifted = kStateMask << kStateShift,
56	        kStateThinOrUnlocked = 0,
57	        kStateFat = 1,
58	        kStateHash = 2,
59	        kStateForwardingAddress = 3,
60	        kStateForwardingAddressShifted = kStateForwardingAddress << kStateShift,
61	        kStateForwardingAddressOverflow = (1 + kStateMask - kStateForwardingAddress) << kStateShift,
62	
63	        // Read barrier bit.
64	        kReadBarrierStateShift = kThinLockCountSize + kThinLockCountShift,
65	        kReadBarrierStateMask = (1 << kReadBarrierStateSize) - 1,
66	        kReadBarrierStateMaskShifted = kReadBarrierStateMask << kReadBarrierStateShift,
67	        kReadBarrierStateMaskShiftedToggled = ~kReadBarrierStateMaskShifted,
68	
69	        // Mark bit.
70	        kMarkBitStateShift = kReadBarrierStateSize + kReadBarrierStateShift,
71	        kMarkBitStateMask = (1 << kMarkBitStateSize) - 1,
72	        kMarkBitStateMaskShifted = kMarkBitStateMask << kMarkBitStateShift,
73	        kMarkBitStateMaskShiftedToggled = ~kMarkBitStateMaskShifted,
74	
75	        // GC state is mark bit and read barrier state.
76	        kGCStateSize = kReadBarrierStateSize + kMarkBitStateSize,
77	        kGCStateShift = kReadBarrierStateShift,
78	        kGCStateMaskShifted = kReadBarrierStateMaskShifted | kMarkBitStateMaskShifted,
79	        kGCStateMaskShiftedToggled = ~kGCStateMaskShifted,
80	
81	        // When the state is kHashCode, the non-state bits hold the hashcode.
82	        // Note Object.hashCode() has the hash code layout hardcoded.
83	        kHashShift = 0,
84	        kHashSize = 32 - kStateSize - kReadBarrierStateSize - kMarkBitStateSize,
85	        kHashMask = (1 << kHashSize) - 1,
86	        kMaxHash = kHashMask,
87	
88	        // Forwarding address shift.
89	        kForwardingAddressShift = kObjectAlignmentShift,
90	
91	        kMonitorIdShift = kHashShift,
92	        kMonitorIdSize = kHashSize,
93	        kMonitorIdMask = kHashMask,
94	        kMonitorIdAlignmentShift = 32 - kMonitorIdSize,
95	        kMonitorIdAlignment = 1 << kMonitorIdAlignmentShift,
96	        kMaxMonitorId = kMaxHash
97	    };
98	
99	    enum LockState {
100	        kUnlocked,    // No lock owners.
101	        kThinLocked,  // Single uncontended owner.
102	        kFatLocked,   // See associated monitor.
103	        kHashCode,    // Lock word contains an identity hash.
104	        kForwardingAddress,  // Lock word contains the forwarding address of an object.
105	    };
106	    LockWord(uint32_t val) : value_(val) {}
107	    LockState GetState();
108	    Monitor FatLockMonitor();
109	    uint32_t ThinLockOwner();
110	private:
111	    uint32_t value_;
112	};
113	
114	} // namespace art
115	
116	#endif // ANDROID_ART_RUNTIME_LOCK_WORD_H_
117

input.purpose: ART 运行时对象模型核心实现，负责镜像和管理 Android Runtime (ART) 的内部数据结构，包括 Java 对象、类、方法、线程、堆空间等关键运行时组件，用于从 coredump 中解析和分析 ART 运行时状态
responsibilities: 镜像 ART 运行时核心数据结构的内存布局，支持跨 Android SDK 版本 (SDK 23-37) 的结构偏移量适配
提供 Runtime 单例访问接口，管理 Heap、ThreadList、ClassLinker、JavaVM、JIT 等全局运行时组件
实现 Java 对象模型：Object、Class、Array、String、DexCache 等 mirror 命名空间下的类型
实现 ArtMethod 和 ArtField，解析方法元数据、访问标志、DEX 索引、入口点地址等
管理线程状态：Thread、ThreadList、线程栈帧 (QuickFrame/ShadowFrame)、寄存器状态
实现 GC 堆空间管理：Heap、ContinuousSpace、DiscontinuousSpace、各类 Space 子类 (ImageSpace、MallocSpace、RegionSpace 等)
支持栈回溯：StackVisitor 遍历 Java 栈帧，解析方法调用链和局部变量
实现类加载器和 DEX 文件管理：ClassLinker、DexCache、类型解析、方法解析
处理同步和锁机制：LockWord、Monitor、MonitorPool，解析对象锁状态和持有者线程
支持 JNI 引用表：JavaVMExt、JNIEnvExt、IndirectReferenceTable (全局/弱全局引用)
实现 JIT 编译支持：Jit、JitCodeCache、OatQuickMethodHeader、代码缓存查询
提供跨架构支持：arch 子目录包含 ARM/ARM64/x86/x86_64/RISCV64 的寄存器和栈帧布局
实现 OAT 文件和栈映射解析：OatFile、StackMap、CodeInfo、优化代码的 PC 到 DEX 映射

key_classes.name: Runtime
key_classes.description: ART 运行时单例，持有全局运行时组件的引用，提供 Current() 静态方法访问

key_classes.name: mirror::Object
key_classes.description: 所有 Java 对象的基类，包含 klass 指针和 monitor 锁字段

key_classes.name: mirror::Class
key_classes.description: Java 类的元数据镜像，包含字段、方法、父类、接口、访问标志等

key_classes.name: ArtMethod
key_classes.description: Java 方法的元数据表示，包含声明类、访问标志、DEX 索引、入口点等

key_classes.name: ArtField
key_classes.description: Java 字段的元数据表示，支持访问实例字段和静态字段值

key_classes.name: Thread
key_classes.description: ART 线程对象，包含线程状态、ID、栈指针、JNI 环境等

key_classes.name: gc::Heap
key_classes.description: GC 堆管理器，维护连续和非连续空间列表

key_classes.name: space::Space
key_classes.description: 堆空间基类，定义 Space 类型 (ImageSpace、MallocSpace、RegionSpace 等)

key_classes.name: ClassLinker
key_classes.description: 类加载器和链接器，管理 DexCache 和类型解析

key_classes.name: StackVisitor
key_classes.description: 栈遍历器，用于回溯 Java 栈帧和方法调用链

interfaces.name: Runtime::Current()
interfaces.description: 获取 ART Runtime 单例实例，是访问运行时状态的入口点

interfaces.name: Object::GetClass()
interfaces.description: 从 Java 对象获取其 Class 元数据

interfaces.name: Class::GetDescriptor()
interfaces.description: 获取类的描述符字符串 (如 'Ljava/lang/String;')

interfaces.name: Thread::GetState()
interfaces.description: 获取线程状态 (Running, Waiting, Blocked 等)

interfaces.name: Heap::GetContinuousSpaces()
interfaces.description: 获取所有连续内存空间列表

interfaces.name: StackVisitor::WalkStack()
interfaces.description: 遍历线程栈帧，构建 JavaFrame 列表

interfaces.name: ArtField::Get32/Get64/GetObject()
interfaces.description: 从对象实例读取字段值 (支持基本类型和引用类型)

dependencies: api/memory_ref.h (MemoryRef 基类，提供内存访问抽象)
android.h (Android 版本检测和 SDK 监听器)

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
已完成对 `android/art/runtime/` 模块的深度分析。这是 OpenCoreAnalysisKit 的核心模块，负责镜像 AOSP ART 运行时的内部数据结构，用于从 coredump 中解析 Android 应用的运行时状态。

**核心设计特点:**

1. **MemoryRef 架构**: 所有类都继承自 `api::MemoryRef`，使用偏移量表 (`*_OffsetTable`) 实现跨 SDK 版本的结构体布局适配

2. **版本兼容性**: 通过 `Init23()` 到 `Init37()` 系列函数，支持 Android 6.0 到 Android 14+ 的所有 ART 版本

3. **缓存机制**: 使用 `QUICK_CACHE` 宏实现惰性加载和缓存，避免重复内存读取

4. **镜像对象模型**: `mirror` 命名空间下的类 (Object、Class、Array、String) 精确对应 AOSP 的 Java 对象内存布局

5. **多架构支持**: `arch/` 子目录为 ARM/ARM64/x86/x86_64/RISCV64 提供寄存器定义和栈帧信息

该模块是整个 coredump 分析系统的基础层，上层命令 (如 `class`、`thread`、`space`) 都依赖它来访问和解析 ART 运行时数据。
